source.title: Serotonin neurons in the dorsal raphe mediate the anticataplectic action of orexin neurons by reducing amygdala activity.
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Direct optogenetic and chemogenetic causal evidence in mouse narcolepsy model. Identifies the DRN-serotonin -> amygdala projection as the route through which orexin's anticataplectic effect propagates. Strongly relevant to MECH-281 and to the cross-link with the 5-HT goal-pipeline gain cluster (MECH-186/187/188). Mapping fidelity is high; transfer risk is the standard rodent-to-architecture concern.
failure_signatures: Optogenetic inhibition of DRN-5HT terminals in the amygdala blocks the anticataplectic effect of orexin signaling -- so a model that locates the entire override authority in orexin alone (without 5-HT mediation) is incomplete.

Chemogenetic activation of amygdala increased cataplexy; chemogenetic inhibition decreased it -- the amygdala output gain is causally bidirectional, consistent with MECH-281's prediction that the override scales rather than silences.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Optogenetic activation of dorsal raphe serotonin neuron terminals in the amygdala (but not in REM-atonia nuclei) suppresses cataplexy-like episodes in orexin-deficient mice. Chemogenetic inhibition of the amygdala reduces cataplexy; activation increases it. Optogenetic inhibition of 5-HT terminals in amygdala blocks the anticataplectic effect of orexin signaling in DRN serotonin neurons. Therefore, DRN serotonin neurons mediate orexin's anticataplectic action by downregulating amygdala activity.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-281 says the override authority gates BLA/CeA arbitration. Hasegawa locates the proximal pathway: orexin -> DRN-5HT -> amygdala. This adds a layer to MECH-281: the override_signal does not modulate amygdala directly; it acts through (or in parallel with) a 5-HT relay. This is consistent with the SD-037 plan that 5-HT (MECH-186/187/188), GABA (SD-036), and orexin (MECH-281, SD-037) form a three-regulator scheme, with overlapping rather than disjoint targets.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Hasegawa data is specifically about cataplexy-like episodes triggered by chocolate in mice; it does not directly speak to the broader motor-coupling failures MECH-281 covers. Also, the 5-HT mediation is one route -- other orexin -> amygdala paths (direct OX1R/OX2R signaling) are not excluded. Treat as strong evidence that the orexin -> amygdala motor-coupling axis exists and is gain-modulated, with 5-HT as one (not sole) mediator.
mapping.source_context: Mouse, orexin-KO model, optogenetic and chemogenetic manipulation, chocolate-trigger cataplexy paradigm.

confidence_components.notes: PNAS, Sakurai/Mieda labs (canonical orexin neuroscience). Mapping is high because the experiment dissociates the proximal pathway, which is the level of detail MECH-281 needs. Slightly below Burgess because Burgess is the lesion-level proof that the path matters at all; Hasegawa is the next-step refinement.
